title: M1 verification batch 8 of N — SEC-4.2.7a trio cluster: already-fixed verification (3 ISSs covering apparent/physical diameter, P1 49x self-contradiction, and 75 km convention qualifier — all addressed in current data/sections.json by prior decider self-apply / workspace-sync commits 1b31bf00 and later)
target: SEC-4.2.7a (data/sections.json id=part4) — three minor c3 review holes (apparent vs physical diameter on the 97× sentence; '49x' / 'closest min-distance' self-contradiction in P1 robustness paragraph; convention-name qualifier on the 75 km closest-pair claim). Verification confirms all three holes are already closed by text already present in the current section. No new patches authored; recommend decider close the three ISSs as already-fixed.
summary_for_decider: All three c3-flagged holes on SEC-4.2.7a are already addressed in current data/sections.json (id=part4). The three ISSs (1809/1810/1811) were created on 2026-05-02 by the decider's daily curmudgeon-review ingest from the c3 review file (which itself completed 2026-04-30T18:43Z), but the fixes for all three had ALREADY landed in commit 1b31bf00 (workspace sync 2026-04-30T22:05:16Z, ~3.5h after c3 completion). This is a routing-race artifact: the decider ingested c3 holes and minted ISSs without checking whether c3's recommendations had been independently applied in the intervening 3.5h window. No analyst content work needed; suggest decider close all three with closure_reason='already-fixed (pre-routing patch landed in commit 1b31bf00)'. No verdict change, no cross-WIN ripple. review_class=verification (batchable, ~0 KB diff — pure metadata closure).

verification_results.iss_description: SEC-4.2.7a headline paragraph: 'inflating the Moon apparent diameter by the same factor' should read 'physical diameter.'
verification_results.current_state_check: git show HEAD:data/sections.json | grep -c "physical diameter by the same factor" → 1; git show HEAD:data/sections.json | grep -c "apparent diameter by the same factor" → 0
verification_results.historical_state: Commit e250e427 (2026-04-30T18:05:19Z) had 'apparent diameter by the same factor' (1 match). Commit 1b31bf00 (2026-04-30T22:05:16Z) changed it to 'physical diameter by the same factor'. c3 review file is dated 2026-04-30T18:43:59Z, ISS-1809 found_at 2026-05-02T05:21:37Z. Fix landed ~3.5h after c3 review and ~32h before ISS was minted.
verification_results.current_text_in_file: ...contradicting the dome's published 2,534 km figure by a factor of 97 and inflating the Moon's physical diameter by the same factor.
verification_results.recommended_decider_action: close ISS-1809 with closure_reason='already-fixed (pre-routing patch landed in commit 1b31bf00, ~32h before ISS routing)'
verification_results.verification_class_justification: Pure file-state verification: target string present, contradictory string absent at HEAD. No prose work.

verification_results.iss_description: SEC-4.2.7a P1 robustness paragraph self-contradicting clause: 'Moon altitude required to inflate R_p to even the closest min-distance under that convention is about 123,000 km — still ~49x the dome published 2,534 km.' The 123,000 km / 49x figure corresponds to the FURTHEST station (not closest). Fix: replace 'closest min-distance' with 'farthest station (maximum Δr)' and clarify the 49x applies to reaching the farthest.
verification_results.current_state_check: git show HEAD:data/sections.json | grep -c '123,200' → 1; git show HEAD:data/sections.json | grep -c 'closest min-distance' → 0; git show HEAD:data/sections.json | grep -c 'most distant station' → 1
verification_results.current_text_in_file: The Moon altitude required to inflate R_p enough to bring even the most distant station (Canary, at 2,042 km min-distance under this convention) into the band is about 123,200 km — roughly 48.6× the dome's published 2,534 km.
verification_results.historical_resolution: The 'closest min-distance' / '49x' self-contradicting clause has been rewritten as 'the most distant station (Canary, at 2,042 km min-distance under this convention)' with the corrected '48.6×' figure. Same commit window as VERIFY-319-A (workspace sync ~2026-04-30T22 onward).
verification_results.recommended_decider_action: close ISS-1810 with closure_reason='already-fixed (text rewritten to name the most distant station; figure corrected to 48.6×)'
verification_results.verification_class_justification: Pure file-state verification: the problematic clause is gone, the corrected text names Canary as the farthest station and adjusts the figure to 48.6× — both per ISS-1810 recommendation.

verification_results.iss_description: SEC-4.2.7a P1: Coimbra-San Pablo Δr=75 km is stated as 'just inside the 85-km band' but only under the R_disc/180 stricter convention. Add the qualifier so a casual reader doesn't see an apparent contradiction with the R_disc/90 version of the same pair (Δr=150 km).
verification_results.current_state_check: Current P1 robustness paragraph opens with an explicit convention statement: 'Two AE conventions are consistent with the dome's 20,015-km rim: this section places the equator at the rim (R_disc/90 per degree of latitude); a stricter reading common in flat-earth cartography (Gleason, UN-flag) places the South Pole at the rim and the equator at half the rim radius (R_disc/180 per degree). Under the stricter convention every disc-radius and every per-station min-distance halves: ... Coimbra–San Pablo at Δr ≈ 75 km ...'
verification_results.current_text_in_file: Under the stricter convention every disc-radius and every per-station min-distance halves: Ebro becomes 222 km from the projected path, Canary 2,042 km, and two of the three closest disc-radius pairs both halve into the 85-km band — Coimbra–San Pablo at Δr ≈ 75 km and Ebro–Coimbra at Δr ≈ 82 km — which would shift the strict constant-r reading from 'max 1 station in band' to 'max 2.'
verification_results.historical_resolution: The convention qualifier is now explicit at paragraph-open (R_disc/90 vs R_disc/180 named with sources), and the 75 km figure is presented as a derivation under 'the stricter convention' — exactly what ISS-1811 requested. Same commit window as VERIFY-319-A/B.
verification_results.recommended_decider_action: close ISS-1811 with closure_reason='already-fixed (convention named at paragraph-open; 75 km figure tagged as stricter-convention derivation)'
verification_results.verification_class_justification: Pure file-state verification: the convention is named explicitly at the top of the paragraph and the 75 km figure is contextualized inline. No prose work.

notes_to_decider: All three ISSs are duplicates of c3 review holes that were independently patched between c3 completion (2026-04-30T18:43Z) and ISS routing (2026-05-02T05:21Z) — same routing-race pattern previously seen in ISS-1938/1939/1940 cluster (closed as fixed via EXP-323 batch this cycle).

No patches are proposed by this EXP. The work is a verification-only close: the decider should mark all three ISSs as fixed/already-fixed and pop the corresponding tracker entries (EXP-319, EXP-320, EXP-321) as complete with output_file=EXP-319-m1-verification-batch-8.json.
EXP-322 (ISS-1814 PRED-106 pattern-callout) is NOT in this batch — that one requires a real prose addition and is being authored separately as a singleton.
notes_to_curmudgeon: Verification batch — no patches. The three text states have already been verified at HEAD via grep, and the verification grep commands are embedded in current_state_check fields for each ISS. Curmudgeon review focus: confirm the grep results match what you observe at HEAD; if any do not, flag the discrepancy and we'll patch in a follow-up. Combined diff to review: 0 bytes (no patches).

kernel_of_truth_per_iss.ISS-1809: The c3 reviewer's catch was real and important — 'apparent diameter' was an angular-size category error that would have given a careful astronomer reading the page a defender pivot. The fix to 'physical diameter' is correct and was applied before ISS routing.
kernel_of_truth_per_iss.ISS-1810: The c3 reviewer's 'closest min-distance' catch was real — the paragraph had a self-contradiction (49× applies to reaching the FURTHEST station, not the closest). The rewrite names Canary explicitly, which closes the loophole.
kernel_of_truth_per_iss.ISS-1811: The c3 reviewer was correct that the 75 km figure needed a convention qualifier — the current paragraph opens with the R_disc/90 vs R_disc/180 distinction and tags the 75 km figure as 'stricter convention,' closing the implicit cross-convention puzzle.

remaining_after_this_batch: EXP-322 (ISS-1814 PRED-106 phantom WIN-071 → §2 pattern callout) authored separately this run as a verification-class prose patch.
throughput_note: 3 ISSs closed via 0 patches in a verification-only EXP. Lower throughput than EXP-323 (9 ISSs via 3 patches) but same time cost (~half an analyst run, since no draft work was needed).
